observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local DRRM Office be institutionalized and organized according to law by assigning full-time permanent employees thereat to maximize its efficiency and effectiveness.

We recommended that Management:

Employ the HGDG tool for major/flagship programs/projects of the LGU and identify which part of the project cost/expenditure is attributable to the GAD Budget;

Maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through the implementation of GAD related programs and projects in order to attain the objectives for which funds were provided; and

Strictly observe the timelines in the submission of GPB and GAD Accomplishment Report to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2021 AAR

observations.recommendation:
In line with the foregoing observations, we recommended to Management the following:
Submit the following documents:
Authority such as Office Orders or Memoranda, indicating the names of persons who were assigned to COVID-19 facilities directly catering to or in contact with COVID-19 patients, PUIs, and PUMs, and the period of their assignment;
Approved payroll duly acknowledged/signed by the payees; and
Duly verified accomplishment/monitoring reports of employees for the period November 1, 2020 to June 30, 2021
Require the refund of SRA from personnel who are not entitled thereof being not in direct contact or did not cater to COVID-19 patients, PUIs, and PUMs;
Strictly adhere to the provisions of RA No. 11469 of the “Bayanihan to Heal as One Act” and subsequent issuances relative thereof to address the needs on the national emergency arising from the COVID-19 pandemic; and
Support all transactions with valid, complete and proper documents as required under Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) henceforth to direct the Municipal Health Office to report all COVID-19 related cases to DOH for transparency and monitoring purposes.
We recommended that Management submit the lacking supporting documents to ascertain the validity of claim for SRA. Otherwise, said amount should be refunded for want of basis.

We recommended that the Property and Accounting Units undertake the necessary reconciliation procedures as outlined in COA Circular No. 2020-006 to establish the correct balance of the PPE and record the necessary adjustments.

We recommended that Management instruct the GSO to accomplish the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) for all the unserviceable properties subject for disposal.
Further, that the Appraisal and Disposal Committee conduct the appraisal of the unserviceable properties to determine the modes of disposal and the appraised values, if any, of the properties to be disposed to facilitate its immediate disposal in accordance with Section 79 of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 89-296.

We recommended that Management continue to prepare adjusting entries to remove from the PPE accounts the remaining properties costing less than ₱15,000 and transfer to the appropriate inventory/expense/prior period accounts. Likewise, an Inventory Custodian Slip shall be prepared for the issued properties costing less than ₱15,000 and with estimated useful life of more than one year.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a request for authority to write-off the dormant receivable accounts amounting to ₱165,531.00 following the procedures in Sections 8.2 and 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant revert the total amount of stale checks outstanding to the Cash in Bank and Accounts Payable accounts to ensure the proper presentation of the account balances.

We recommended that Management submit certified true copies of the above-mentioned documentary requirements in support of the payment for terminal leave benefits.
We recommended that henceforth Management support all transactions with valid, complete and proper documents as required under Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that the agency require the end-user/implementing units to fill-up all the information required in the preparation of the PPMP under Section 7.3.2 of the RIRR of RA No. 9184 and utilize the GPPB approved format of the PPMP
We recommended that the LCE henceforth direct the BAC to utilize the GPPB approved format of the APP.
We recommended that the agency prepare its PMR in the form prescribed by the GPPB in compliance with Section 12.2 of the RIRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the agency prepare its APCPI and immediately submit a copy to GPPB TSO. Whereas, APCPI results are among the required documents to be submitted to GPPB for the grant of Performance-Based Bonus every year starting CY 2015.

In line with the foregoing observation, we recommended to Management the following:
Submit through electronic mail a copy of its APP, PMR and APCPI results to the GPPB, in both Microsoft Excel and PDF File; and
Post the APP and PMR in the agency website or in a conspicuous place reserved for this purpose in the premises of the Municipality and submit a certification indicating that it has complied with either of the said posting requirements to the GPPB.

We recommended that the LCE direct the BAC to keep abreast of updates in Philippine Bidding Documents (PBDs) and Required Forms and thus specifically ensure that Philippine Bidding Documents made available to prospective bidders are in accordance with the 6th edition of the PBDs.

We recommended that the agency conduct the pre-bid conference twelve (12) calendar days before the deadline for the submission and receipt of bids, but not earlier than seven (7) calendar days from the PhilGEPS posting of the Invitation to Bid (ITB) or bidding documents in accordance with Section 22.2 of the RIRR of RA No. 9184.
We recommended that the Municipality refrain from entering into contract prior to the issuance of NOA, as NOA shall form as an integral part of the contract.
We recommended that the Municipality schedule procurement activities in accordance with the timelines set forth in the RIRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the Municipality impose liquidated damages corresponding to the number of days delayed pursuant to Section 68, Rule XXII of the RIRR of RA 9184, if no justifiable reason was communicated by the supplier and approved by the LCE.

We recommended that Management:
Submit the lacking supporting documents;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness of supporting documents in the payment of infrastructure projects; and
Direct the BAC to perform its function in strict compliance with what is mandated per RA 9184 and its IRR to impress upon stakeholders that procurement activities conducted are aboveboard.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to subject to Retention Money all payments for infrastructure projects equivalent to at least 10 per cent of the 50 per cent of the value of works and impose additional retention money for remaining value of works  in case it is not satisfactorily done and not on schedule and to allow substitution only if the conditions are met pursuant to Section 6 of the Annex E of the IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

We recommended that the Municipality under the direction of the LCE:
Ensure that Certificate of Final Acceptance for the infrastructure projects be issued only upon the lapse of one-year period from date of its completion;
Direct the Municipal Engineering Office through its Department Head and inspectorate team or counterpart thereto to conduct necessary and thorough determination of any resulting defects occurring within the periods mentioned, defects liability period to the structural defects period for the filing of appropriate claims with the contractor and parties liable in accordance with Sections 62.2.2 and 62.2.3 of the 2016 Revised IRR of the R.A. No. 9184; and
Provide the Audit Team with the result of the preceding recommended action on the determination of any resulting defects during the defects liability period until the structural defects period.

We recommended that Management:
Submit the lacking supporting documents;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness of supporting documents in the payment of infrastructure projects; and
Direct the BAC to perform its function in strict compliance with what is mandated per RA 9184 and its IRR to impress upon stakeholders that procurement activities conducted are aboveboard.

We recommended that Management maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF to cover all the four thematic areas of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management in order to enhance the preparedness of the Municipality against disasters and avoid, lessen or limit their adverse impacts to the community.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer strictly observe the proper charging of expenditures to the LDRRMF to ensure that the funds are utilized in accordance with its intended purposes.

We commended Management for formulating the PSCP as required in the NDRRMC Memorandum No. 33, s. 2018 and No. 57, s. 2020. Further, we recommended Management to continually improve and update the PSCP in response to the changes and demands of time.

We recommended that Management continue to improve practices in programming, prioritizing and implementing programs and projects to maximize the utilization of the Fund in attaining desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes for the benefit of the community.

We commended the Municipality and the Local School Board for significantly utilizing the SEF in accordance with existing guidelines in the utilization of SEF especially on allowable priority programs, projects and activities responsive to the current health emergency.
Further, we recommended that they continue to improve their utilization of the SEF and to provide for equitable budget for all the priority programs, projects and activities allowable as SEF expenditures.
We commended Management’s commitment in reducing the volume of waste using innovative techniques/means and in generating revenue from waste materials.

We recommended that the agency revisit its current organizational structure of the SWMB and assess its conformity with the required structure under the RA No. 9003.  Otherwise, provide explanation why the structure as required could not be met.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the coordination of MSWMB and Barangay Solid Waste Management Board (BSWMB) to ensure that all barangays are provided with MRFs that are properly maintained and fully operational.

We commended the Local Chief Executive, the Municipal Accountant and staff for the early submission of the CY 2021 Year-End Financial Statements and other financial reports, prepared in conformity with IPSAS.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant continue the timely submission of the year-end financial statements, together with its supporting schedules, to enable the timely verification thereof by the Auditor.
We recommended that Management further improve its performance in the submission of the Monthly Trial Balances and the Quarterly Financial Statements.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management:
Require the concerned Accountable Officer to submit the accounts and financial reports to the Municipal Treasurer pursuant to the timelines of the abovementioned existing law, rules and regulations;
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Municipal Accounting Office all the disbursements vouchers, payrolls, official receipts, including the corresponding reports for each month on or before the fifth day of the ensuing month or pursuant to the abovementioned pertinent provisions of rules and regulations, as applicable; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the timelines on the recording and submission of accounts to the Auditor within ten days of the ensuing month.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to hasten the preparation of the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements and provide the Audit Team with a copy thereof, including the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) for the recording of the adjustments/reconciling items, for review and verification.

We recommended that Management submit the lacking documentary requirements and henceforth to strictly observe the submission of the documentary requirements including the Omnibus Sworn Statements in the prescribed format for the Emergency Procurements under the Bayanihan Act. Likewise, for Management to transact only with legally, financially and technically capable suppliers/contractors for Emergency Procurements under the Bayanihan Act.

We recommended that the Municipality strictly adhere to the mandatory posting on the GPPB Online Portal of information relative to Emergency Procurements under the Bayanihan Act to ensure that accountability and transparency in the procurement is upheld.

We recommended that the Municipality, through various process owners involved in the procurement process of acknowledging receipt of and issuance of goods procured, ensure that prescribed forms are properly accomplished with information as required therein. Further, for Management to explain the circumstances surrounding the two payments to same supplier supported with same PO number and pictures.

We recommended that Management:

Compel the MSWD Officer to fast track the submission of the Relief Distribution Lists indicating the names of recipients and their signatures or, at the very least, signed by the Punong Barangay as recipient in behalf of the constituents, otherwise, non-submission thereof would warrant the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance; and 

Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all claims against government funds shall be supported with complete documentation to protect the interests of the government and to prevent unwarranted disbursements of public funds.

We recommended that Management, henceforth, strictly abide with the terms and conditions stated in the Memorandum of Agreement, through the immediate distribution of subsidies to identified eligible beneficiaries as well as the liquidation of funds received within the timeline, to achieve the objectives of the Emergency Subsidy Program of the government.

We recommended that the Management:

Strictly comply with the requirement on submission of the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances within the reglementary period, duly supported with required documents; and

Disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements relevant information regarding in-kind donations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant revert the total amount of stale checks outstanding to the Cash-in Bank and Accounts Payable accounts to ensure the proper presentation of the account balances.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to hasten the preparation of the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements and provide the Audit Team with a copy thereof, including the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) for the recording of the adjustments/reconciling items, for review and verification.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a request for authority to write-off the dormant receivable accounts amounting to ₱165,531 following the procedures in Sections 8.2 and 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that Management prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) as of December 31, 2020 in the prescribed format and signed by the members of the Inventory Committee. Henceforth, Management strictly observe the submission of the RPCPPE within the timeline which is not later than January 31 of each year.

We recommended that Management instruct the GSO to accomplish the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) for all the unserviceable properties subject for disposal.  

Further, create an Appraisal and Disposal Committee to conduct the appraisal of the unserviceable properties to determine the modes of disposal and the appraised values, if any, of the properties to be disposed to facilitate its immediate disposal in accordance with Section 79 of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 89-296.

We recommended that Management prepare an adjusting entry to remove from the PPE accounts properties costing less than ₱15,000 and transfer to the appropriate inventory/expense/prior period accounts. Likewise, an Inventory Custodian Slip shall be prepared for properties costing less than ₱15,000, but which can be used for more than one year, that have already been issued.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the non-current portion of Loans Payable-Domestic, which for this year amounted to ₱14.65 million, and, henceforth to observe the proper classification of current and long-term liabilities for fairer presentation of the Statement of Financial Position to provide more accurate information to the users thereof.

We commended Management for the substantial implementation of the projects under the 20 per cent Development Fund and recommend that it continue to improve practices in programming, prioritizing and implementing programs and projects to maximize the utilization of the Fund in attaining desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes for the benefit of the community.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly observe the proper charging of expenditures to the 30 percent Quick Response Fund and 70 percent Mitigation Fund to ensure that the funds are utilized in accordance with its intended purpose.

We commended the Municipality and the Local School Board for significantly utilizing the SEF in accordance with existing guidelines in the utilization of SEF.  We, however, also enjoin the Municipality and the Local School Board to consider providing for other allowable priority programs, projects and activities responsive to the current health emergency.

We recommended that Management establish the Municipal Internal Audit Office following the guidelines set forth under DBM Local Budget Circular (LBC) No. 110 in the organizational structure and staffing of office to further improve and strengthen the internal control system of the Municipality. Pending creation of the Office, Management can designate LGU personnel to perform the functions of the IAS/U.

And other officials concerned to exert more efforts to collect the rentals due from the delinquent market stall occupants, and if necessary avail the civil remedies provided by the present Revenue Code, in order to generate additional income that will be used for the implementation of programs, projects and activities of the Municipality; and

observations.reason:
We acknowledge the Audit Team’s observations. However, we would like to manifest that our Office is having difficulty in submitting the financial reports within the prescribed deadline due to the following reasons:
1.The Accounting office normally receives the collection reports from the Treasurer’s Office 5 days after the end of the month. Given that  the deadline of submission of submission of the reports to COA is 10 days after the end of the month, the Accounting office has only 5 days to segregate, review and record the reports. Further, it was observed that the collectors were only preparing and/or submitting their reports when stubs issued to them were completely used, rather than on a daily/weekly basis, thus making the preparation of abstract of collections more difficult.
2.Remittances to GSIS, Pag-ibig, PHIC and BIR also fall on the 10th day of the following month. The Accounting office needs to prioritize the processing of these remittances to avoid surcharges and penalties.
3. At times, some of our staff have to attend meetings and seminars.
4.In addition, the Office is also processing voluminous disbursement vouchers on a daily basis and given our limited personnel, submission of accounts within the deadline becomes improbable, if not impossible. Nonetheless, rest assured that we will implement measures to comply with the audit recommendations.

Take appropriate immediate remedial measures, such as augmenting the accountant’s office by hiring additional qualified personnel to specifically prepare the monthly BRS and/or allowing the personnel in-charge of said BRS to render overtime subject to availability of funds, in order to submit the same to the auditor on the mandated deadline.

We recommended that the Accountant prepare the necessary journal entries torecord the adjustments affecting the balance of the account and require the Disbursing Officers concerned to reconcile/update/correct their Cash in Bank Registers with thereconciling items/adjustments to reflect the correct balance of Cash in Bank-LCCA account intheir custody.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare a request for the writing-off of the dormant receivable accounts amounting to ₱99,181 from COA, supporting the said request with the required documents as enumerated in Item 8.3 of the COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and the Property and Supply Officer to continue their process of reconciling the PPELC with the RPCPPE, respectively, and immediately effect the necessary adjustments, if any.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the final reconciliation and accounting of these account balances and prepare the DVs together with the supporting schedule for the Municipal Treasurer to issue a check payable either to the Source Agency or direct to the National Treasury. Those funds which are considered as special purpose funds shall be retained and those balances for which original releases is in the nature of financial assistance or subsidy and for which the agreement do not mention of the disposition of the unused balance, may be reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the Municipality and future utilization thereof subject to the usual accounting, auditing and budgeting laws and regulations.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to make final reconciliation and accounting of these account balances and prepare the DVs together with the supporting schedule for the Municipal Treasurer to issue a check payable either to the recipients LGUs, or returned to Source Agency. We recommended that Management adhere strictly with the provision of Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and submit regularly to the Audit Team the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF, prepared by the Municipal Accountant using the proposed format by the Audit Team in “Annex A”, on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council and the Municipal Development Council.

We recommended that Management implement BUB projects and programs in the most timely manner possible in order that the benefits to be derived therefrom may be enjoyed by the intended beneficiaries at the earliest possible time. We also recommended that Management secure certificates of completion and/or acceptance for the fully implemented projects in order that the corresponding fund for the project be properly adjusted and reflect the correct balance in the books.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
a. Intensify the tax collection campaign to maximize collection of RPT due to the Municipal Government in order to increase its revenue and have extra funds available to finance the delivery of basic services and facilities; and
b. Send demand letters to collect taxes from delinquent taxpayers and in case of failure to collect, avail of the remedies through levy on real property or judicial actions provided under the Local Government Code of 1991.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct theMunicipal Treasurer:
a.	And other officials concerned to exert more efforts to collect the rentals due from the delinquent market stall occupants, and if necessary avail the civil remedies provided by the present Revenue Code, in order to generate additional income that will be used for the implementation of programs, projectsand activities of the Municipality; and
b.	To periodically monitor the rental payment of the stallholders and demand immediate payment when due and demandable to avoid arrears and accumulation of amount due.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
The Municipal Treasurer to provide the Municipal Accountant with the List of Delinquent Market Stallholders with amount due and penalties before the end of each year to facilitate timely recording of appropriate receivable and income accounts; and
The Municipal Accountant to recognize the total unpaid rentals, business taxes, surcharges and interests as of December 31, 2018 in the books of the Municipality to the appropriate receivable and income/equity account, in compliance with the provisions of PPSAS 13 in order to present fairly the financial statements of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the Monthly Trial Balance and Quarterly Financial Statements with complete Schedules and Annexes for CY 2018 within the prescribed timeframe in accordance with the abovementioned existing rules and regulations andhenceforth, to prepare and submit the same without delays;
Take appropriate immediate remedial measures, such as augmenting the Municipal Accountant’s Office of personnel from other Departments, especially during report preparations and/or allowing them to render overtime, subject to the availability of funds, in order to comply with the timely preparation and submission of these mandatory Financial reports, pursuant to mandated deadlines; and
Allow the Municipal Accountant to recommend qualified plantilla personnel of the Municipal Accounting Office to attend Trainings or Seminars relative to their works, to equip them with the required knowledge and skills in order to improve their performance.

We recommended that Management:

To stop the practice of giving different rates to JOs having the same position in the Agency in order not to createinequality in the work force of the LGU; and
